mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) across 100+ rate decline observations, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE increases are driven by seasonal adjustments.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the interaction term loses significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A consecutive 2-month decline in the industrial production index during periods of high inflation indicates supply-side bottlenecks that compress corporate margins and generate negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 50% of triggered instances during high inflation periods, or the signal reverses direction when supply shocks are temporary.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate cut exceeding 25 basis points signals aggressive monetary accommodation that rapidly boosts equity risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines > 0.25% in a single month
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of rate cut instances, or the market prices in the cut prior to the announcement eliminating the forward return effect.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment rates indicates structural labor market deterioration that suppresses earnings growth expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence pattern fails to predict negative 60-day returns in > 60% of historical occurrences, or the signal is fully explained by concurrent monetary policy changes.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reflects underlying economic expansion that supports corporate revenue forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows zero correlation with 20-day forward returns across 50+ quarterly observations, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and retail sales during periods of elevated interest rates signals demand destruction that rapidly penalizes equity valuations over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of triggered instances, or the contraction pattern fails to predict returns when accompanied by fiscal stimulus announcements.
